controls.notes: In CIS Benchmark, the requirement is manual, because of GPG keys for 3rd party repositories. But, add the rule ensure_fedora_gpgkey_installed to the profile because the requirement 1.2.1.2 adds ensure_gpgcheck_globally_activated which requires GPG key checking. If the Fedora GPG key wouldn't be installed, people won't be able to install any RPM package using dnf.
controls.title: Ensure GPG keys are configured (Manual)
Ensure gpgcheck is configured (Automated)
Ensure repo_gpgcheck is globally activated (Manual)
Ensure package manager repositories are configured (Manual)
Ensure weak dependencies are disabled in dnf (Manual)
Ensure updates, patches, and additional security software are installed (Manual)

Ensure no unconfined services exist (Manual)

Ensure SETroubleshoot is not installed (Automated)

Ensure nftables is installed (Automated)
Ensure a single firewall configuration utility is in use (Automated)
Ensure firewalld drops unnecessary services and ports (Manual)
Ensure firewalld loopback traffic is configured (Automated)

controls.notes:
RHEL systems use firewalld for firewall management. Although nftables is the default
back-end for firewalld, it is not recommended to use nftables directly when firewalld
is in use. When using firewalld the base chains are installed by default.
controls.title: Ensure nftables base chains exist (Automated)
Ensure nftables established connections are configured (Manual)

controls.notes:
RHEL systems use firewalld for firewall management. Although nftables is the default
back-end for firewalld, it is not recommended to use nftables directly when firewalld
is in use.
controls.title: Ensure nftables default deny firewall policy (Automated)

controls.notes:
RHEL systems use firewalld for firewall management. Although nftables is the default
back-end for firewalld, it is not recommended to use nftables directly when firewalld
is in use.
controls.title: Ensure nftables loopback traffic is configured (Automated)
Ensure access to /etc/ssh/sshd_config is configured (Automated)
Ensure access to SSH private host key files is configured (Automated)
Ensure access to SSH public host key files is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd Ciphers are configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd KexAlgorithms is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd MACs are configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd access is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd Banner is configured (Automated)

controls.notes:
The requirement gives an example of 45 seconds, but is flexible about the values. It is only
necessary to ensure there is a timeout configured in alignment to the site policy.
controls.title: Ensure sshd ClientAliveInterval and ClientAliveCountMax are configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd DisableForwarding is enabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd GSSAPIAuthentication is disabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd HostbasedAuthentication is disabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd IgnoreRhosts is enabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd LoginGraceTime is configured (Automated)

controls.notes:
The CIS benchmark is not opinionated about which loglevel is selected here. Here, this
profile uses VERBOSE by default, as it allows for the capture of login and logout activity
as well as key fingerprints.
controls.title: Ensure sshd LogLevel is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd MaxAuthTries is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd MaxStartups is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd MaxSessions is configured (Automated)
Ensure sshd PermitEmptyPasswords is disabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd PermitRootLogin is disabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd PermitUserEnvironment is disabled (Automated)
Ensure sshd UsePAM is enabled (Automated)
Ensure sudo is installed (Automated)
Ensure sudo commands use pty (Automated)
Ensure sudo log file exists (Automated)
Ensure users must provide password for escalation (Automated)
Ensure re-authentication for privilege escalation is not disabled globally (Automated)
Ensure sudo timestamp_timeout is configured (Automated)

controls.notes:
Members of "wheel" or GID 0 groups are checked by default if the group option is not set for
pam_wheel.so module. The recommendation states the group should be empty to reinforce the
use of "sudo" for privileged access. Therefore, members of these groups should be manually
checked or a different group should be informed.
controls.title: Ensure access to the su command is restricted (Automated)

controls.notes: It is necessary a new rule to ensure PAM package is updated.
controls.title: Ensure latest version of pam is installed (Automated)

controls.notes: It is necessary a new rule to ensure authselect package is updated.
controls.title: Ensure latest version of authselect is installed (Automated)

controls.notes: It is necessary a new rule to ensure libpwquality package is updated.
controls.title: Ensure latest version of libpwquality is installed (Automated)

controls.notes:
This rule verifies that the active authselect profile includes the required PAM modules:
pam_pwquality.so, pam_pwhistory.so, pam_faillock.so, and pam_unix.so in both system-auth
and password-auth files. The rule checks the authselect profile source files directly,
not the symlinked files in /etc/pam.d/. Other rules ensure these modules are properly
configured with correct options.
controls.title: Ensure active authselect profile includes pam modules (Automated)

controls.notes: This requirement is also indirectly satisfied by the requirement 5.3.3.1.
controls.title: Ensure pam_faillock module is enabled (Automated)

controls.notes: This requirement is also indirectly satisfied by the requirement 5.3.3.2.
controls.title: Ensure pam_pwquality module is enabled (Automated)

controls.notes:
The module is properly enabled by the rules mentioned in related_rules.
Requirements in 5.3.3.3 use these rules.
controls.title: Ensure pam_pwhistory module is enabled (Automated)
Ensure pam_unix module is enabled (Automated)
Ensure password failed attempts lockout is configured (Automated)

controls.notes:
The policy also accepts value 0, which means the locked accounts should be manually unlocked
by an administrator. However, it also mentions that using value 0 can facilitate a DoS
attack to legitimate users.
controls.title: Ensure password unlock time is configured (Automated)
Ensure password failed attempts lockout includes root account (Automated)
Ensure password number of changed characters is configured (Automated)
Ensure password length is configured (Automated)

controls.notes:
This requirement is expected to be manual. However, in previous versions of the policy
it was already automated the configuration of "minclass" option. Rules related to other
options are informed in related_rules. In short, minclass=4 alone can achieve the same
result achieved by the combination of the other 4 options mentioned in the policy.
controls.title: Ensure password complexity is configured (Manual)
Ensure password same consecutive characters is configured (Automated)
Ensure password maximum sequential characters is configured (Automated)
Ensure password dictionary check is enabled (Automated)
Ensure password quality is enforced for the root user (Automated)

controls.notes:
Although mentioned in the section 5.3.3.3, there is no explicit requirement to configure
retry option of pam_pwhistory. If come in the future, the rule accounts_password_pam_retry
can be used.
controls.title: Ensure password history remember is configured (Automated)
Ensure password history is enforced for the root user (Automated)

controls.notes:
pam_pwhistory is enabled via authselect feature, as required in 5.3.2.4. The
feature automatically set "use_authok" option. In any case, we don't have a rule to check
this option specifically.
controls.title: Ensure pam_pwhistory includes use_authtok (Automated)

controls.notes: The rule more specifically used in this requirement also satify the requirement 5.3.2.5.
controls.title: Ensure pam_unix does not include nullok (Automated)

controls.notes:
Usage of pam_unix.so module together with "remember" option is deprecated and is not
recommened by this policy. Instead, it should be used remember option of pam_pwhistory
module, as required in 5.3.3.3.1. See here for more details about pam_unix.so:
https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1778929
controls.title: Ensure pam_unix does not include remember (Automated)

controls.notes: Changes in logindefs mentioned in this requirement are more specifically covered by 5.4.1.4
controls.title: Ensure pam_unix includes a strong password hashing algorithm (Automated)

controls.notes:
In RHEL 9 pam_unix is enabled by default in all authselect profiles already with the
use_authtok option set. In any case, we don't have a rule to check this option specifically,
like in 5.3.3.3.3.
controls.title: Ensure pam_unix includes use_authtok (Automated)
Ensure password expiration is configured (Automated)

Ensure password expiration warning days is configured (Automated)
Ensure strong password hashing algorithm is configured (Automated)
Ensure inactive password lock is configured (Automated)
